DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT U.S. BANCORP'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF PROCESS AS TO SPECIALLY APPEARING DEFENDANT TO BE HEARD AT 9:30 AM ON THE LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR. JUDGE PRO TEM: ANTHONY SCHOENBERG; CLERK L. FONG; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)
RENEWED REPLY IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE FILED BY DEFENDANT U.S. BANCORP

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT U.S. BANCORP'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF PROCESS AS TO SPECIALLY APPEARING DEFENDANT IS GRANTED. (CCP 418.10.) PLAINTIFF HAS NOT MET HER BURDEN OF PROOF TO SHOW THE FACTS REQUISITE TO AN EFFECTIVE SERVICE. (SUMMERS V. MCCLANAHAN (2006) 140 CAL.APP.4TH 403, 413.) PLAINTIFF'S RELIANCE ON A PURPORTED STATEMENT MADE BY MR. FOOTE AT A COURT HEARING HELD ON SEPTEMBER 13, 2011, WHICH PREDATES THE DOE SUBSTITUTION OF DEFENDANT US BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, ND ON AUGUST 22, 2012 BY APPROXIMATELY 11 MONTHS, IS INSUFFICIENT TO DEMONSTRATE EFFECTIVE SERVICE. (PETERSON DECL., 1.) FURTHER, MR. FOOTE STATES THAT HE IS NOT AUTHORIZED TO ACCEPT SERVICE ON BEHALF OF DEFENDANT US BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, ND. (FOOTE DECL., 3.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PAUL MORTON, A U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION EMPLOYEE'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF PROCESSB IS GRANTED. (CCP 418.10.) PLAINTIFF HAS NOT MET HER INITIAL BURDEN OF PROOF TO DEMONSTRATE THAT MINIMUM CONTACTS EXIST BETWEEN MR. MORTON AND CALIFORNIA. (MIHLON V. SUP.CT. (1985) 169 CAL.APP.3D 703, 710.) NEITHER THE COMPLAINT NOR PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION ADDRESSES THE ISSUE OF MINIMUM CONTACTS. FURTHERMORE, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT MET HER BURDEN OF PROOF TO SHOW THE FACTS REQUISITE TO AN EFFECTIVE SERVICE. (SUMMERS V. MCCLANAHAN (2006) 140 CAL.APP.4TH 403, 413.) PLAINTIFF'S RELIANCE ON A PURPORTED STATEMENT MADE BY MR. FOOTE AT A COURT HEARING HELD ON SEPTEMBER 13, 2011, WHICH PREDATES THE DOE SUBSTITUTION OF MR. MORTON ON AUGUST 22, 2012 BY APPROXIMATELY 11 MONTHS, IS INSUFFICIENT TO DEMONSTRATE EFFECTIVE SERVICE. (PETERSON DECL., 1.) FURTHER, MR. FOOTE STATES THAT HE IS NOT AUTHORIZED TO ACCEPT SERVICE ON BEHALF OF MR. MORTON . (FOOTE DECL., 3.) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-22-2013 Continued To Feb-19-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant Paul Motion, A U.S. Bank National Association Employee'S Motion To Quash Service Of Process
Off Calendar Nov-14-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: VICTORIA PETERSON, PLAINTIFF IN PRO PER.
ROBERT FINEMAN, ESQ., 415.957.3210, FOR DEFENDANTS US BANCORP AND PAUL MOTION, A US BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION EMPLOYEE.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Defendant Paul Morton, A U.S. Bank National Association Employee's Motion To Quash Service Of ProcessB IS Granted. (CCP 418.10.) Plaintiff has not met her initial burden of proof to demonstrate that minimum contacts exist between Mr. Morton and California. (Mihlon v. Sup.Ct. (1985) 169 Cal.App.3d 703, 710.) Neither the Complaint nor Plaintiff's opposition addresses the issue of minimum contacts. Furthermore, Plaintiff has not met her burden of proof to show the facts requisite to an effective service. (Summers v. McClanahan (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 403, 413.) Plaintiff's reliance on a purported statement made by Mr. Foote at a court hearing held on September 13, 2011, which predates the doe substitution of Mr. Morton on August 22, 2012 by approximately 11 months, is insufficient to demonstrate effective service. (Peterson Decl., 1.) Further, Mr. Foote states that he is not authorized to accept service on behalf of Mr. Morton . (Foote Decl., 3.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

Defendant U.S. Bancorp'S Motion To Quash Service Of Process As To Specially Appearing Defendant (From Discovery To Law & Motion)
Off Calendar Nov-14-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: VICTORIA PETERSON, PLAINTIFF IN PRO PER.
ROBERT FINEMAN, ESQ., 415.957.3210, FOR DEFENDANTS US BANCORP AND PAUL MOTION, A US BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION EMPLOYEE.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Defendant U.S. Bancorp's Motion To Quash Service Of Process As To Specially Appearing Defendant is Granted. (CCP 418.10.) Plaintiff has not met her burden of proof to show the facts requisite to an effective service. (Summers v. McClanahan (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 403, 413.) Plaintiff's reliance on a purported statement made by Mr. Foote at a court hearing held on September 13, 2011, which predates the doe substitution of Defendant US Bank National Association, ND on August 22, 2012 by approximately 11 months, is insufficient to demonstrate effective service. (Peterson Decl., 1.) Further, Mr. Foote states that he is not authorized to accept service on behalf of Defendant US Bank National Association, ND. (Foote Decl., 3.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

Notice Of Motion To Compel
Off Calendar Apr-02-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Christian Foote, Esq., of Duane Morris LLP, appearing for Defendant and moving party.
Victoria L. Peterson, Tel. 415.730.6475, plaintiff and opposing party appearing on her own behalf.

The matter was discussed. The court adopted the tentative ruling: DEFENDANT U.S. BANCORP's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Form Interrogatories is granted in part, denied in part. In some instances there is no practical benefit to requiring more complete answers. Plaintiff shall supply answers or more complete answers to the following interrogatories: 2.12 section (c) only, 6.6, 6.7 section (a) only, 8.3, 8.4 as to how the amount was calculated only, 8.5, 8.7 as to how the amount was calculated only, 8.8, 9.2, 10.1, 10.3, 11.1, 12.1, 12.2, 12.3, 12.4, 12.6 requires yes or no answer only, 50.1 through 50.6. The motion is denied as to all other interrogatories. No sanctions for either party. Order signed in open court. Pro Tem Judge: Tom Cohen, not reported.
